name: BTC Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from repaired positioning and resumed drift back with the prior direction.
invalidation_condition: Abort if flush-and-reclaim remains too sparse, if reclaims fail to extend beyond a weak bounce, or if the idea only survives as low-trade cosmetic output.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes away from short-term value, quickly reclaims balance, and then continues for a second leg as trapped inventory unwinds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition-heavy intraday tapes often reset positioning violently before resuming prior directional drift. A reclaim-based continuation should be denser than deep-value bottom-picking.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is chosen because reclaim structure is cleaner and less noisy than on AXS while still offering enough range for continuation after the reset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the reclaim only repairs to balance and never resumes, the continuation thesis is wrong and exits will harvest only noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but extension is weak, convert more size to early harvesting before changing the reclaim logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Reasonable hit rate with medium winners when inventory repair transitions into continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC and at least one cross-lane show real trade density with repaired-continuation follow-through after costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade PF mirage, or reclaims that consistently die at first value touch.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad flush/reclaim logic instead of exact candle choreography

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest lane for testing whether flush-and-reclaim can continue rather than just mean revert.
test_lanes.continue_if: Credible density and post-cost second-leg follow-through appear.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Reclaims remain too sparse or die immediately after balance is retaken.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Regular repaired continuations after flushes, with cleaner structure than ETH or AXS.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same repair-of-inventory mechanism generalizes across majors.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH shows the same shape with similar density and coherent structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Only BTC works and ETH breaks the thesis.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but still recognizable repaired continuations.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism survives on a wilder structurally different asset.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS preserves the reclaim-continuation profile with acceptable density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The pattern collapses into random swings.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More volatile but still recognizable flush-reclaim-second-leg behavior.
